methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search mainly returned Chalmers research-publication material, which was not direct policy evidence.
Firecrawl search found Chalmers Library guide pages, a Chalmers student news article, and research-publication pages.
Firecrawl search found official Chalmers Library student, teacher, and ethical-use guide pages.
The Chalmers Library guide linked to the student page, teacher page, ethical-use page, and thesis AI-tool regulations on www.chalmers.se.
Teacher guidance listed intranet resources, but direct fetches from outside the Chalmers network returned a VPN access requirement rather than substantive policy text.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found accessible Chalmers Library guidance for students, teachers, ethical use, and a public Chalmers thesis-work AI-tool regulation page. Intranet AI guidance was recorded as inaccessible because it required VPN access. News and research-publication hits were rejected as non-policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Regulations for the use of AI tools in thesis work

artifacts.claimText: Chalmers Library's student guidance says whether and how AI tools may be used in examinations is decided for each course by the coordinator and examiner, so students should ask at the beginning of each course.
artifacts.normalizedValue: course-level coordinator and examiner decide examination AI-tool use

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Chalmers Library's student guidance connects AI use to plagiarism or ghostwriting risk and says students need to be fully transparent, describe what they did, and cite the AI tool used.
artifacts.normalizedValue: student AI use requires transparency and citation in academic-integrity context

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For thesis work, Chalmers says AI tools may be permitted, but students are required to take full responsibility and make AI-tool use clear and transparent, including how and to what extent the tools were used.
artifacts.normalizedValue: thesis AI-tool use may be permitted with responsibility and transparent disclosure

artifacts.claimText: For thesis work, Chalmers tells students to consult a Chalmers supervisor or relevant collaborator before using AI tools when there are uncertainties, particularly around data privacy and ethical issues.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is up to the coordinator and examiner for each individual course to decide whether and how AI tools are allowed to be used within the framework of the various examinations. Remember to ask at the beginning of each course!
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Chalmers Library says course coordinators and examiners decide whether and how AI tools are allowed in each course's examinations.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: The use of genAI can change depending on the course in question

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Claiming that what someone else has written is your own text without referencing to it correctly is considered as plagiarism. In relation to AI, this can be interpreted as either plagiarism or ghostwriting, so you need to be fully transparent in your use of AI. Describe what you have done and cite correctly, including which AI tool you have used.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Chalmers Library links AI use to plagiarism or ghostwriting risk and tells students to be transparent and cite the AI tool used.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Regulations for the use of AI tools in thesis work
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You are required to assume full responsibility for your work and must be capable of justifying the choices you've made regarding its content. This includes engaging in discussions about and defending the role AI played in shaping your thesis, demonstrating a clear understanding of how it contributed.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Chalmers requires thesis authors to take full responsibility for their work and be able to justify the role AI played.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: You are required to assume full responsibility

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: All students and employees at Chalmers have access to the chatbot Microsoft Copilot. In addition, Chalmers offers Microsoft Copilot Pro licenses to employees who are interested, please contact your immediate manager. Chalmers does not currently have a license for ChatGPT.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Because of this, you should not upload material that contains sensitive or private information, or any material protected by copyright law, for example PDFs of scientific articles that you’ve downloaded from library databases. If you have text intended for a thesis, dissertation, or scientific publication, you should not submit it to a chatbot, as doing so may mean relinquishing your copyright to that material.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Chalmers Library cautions against uploading sensitive, private, copyrighted, or unpublished scholarly material to chatbots.

Approved because the source directly links AI use to transparency, citation, plagiarism, and ghostwriting risk.

artifacts.title: Chalmers University of Technology AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Staged artifacts capture accessible official Chalmers guidance on course-specific AI use, academic-integrity transparency, thesis-work AI-tool responsibility, thesis privacy consultation, Copilot access, and cautions against uploading sensitive, private, copyrighted, or unpublished scholarly material to chatbots.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Intranet resources listed by Chalmers Library could not be used because outside-network fetches returned only a VPN access requirement.
Claims are scoped to accessible official Chalmers Library and public student-thesis pages; news and research-publication pages were rejected as non-policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
